sanskrit:
मन्यसे यदि तच्छक्यं मया द्रष्टुम् इति प्रभो।
योगेश्वर ततो मे त्वं दर्शयात्मानमव्ययम्॥
transliteration: manyase yadi tac chakyaṁ mayā draṣṭum iti prabho | yogeśvara tato me tvaṁ darśayātmānam avyayam ||
literal_meaning: If You think that this is possible for me to see, O Lord, O Master of Yoga, then please show me Your imperishable Self.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna humbly requests Krishna to reveal the cosmic form, acknowledging his own perceptual limits and surrendering the locus of control to the Other.
psychological_state: Healthy regression—trading egoic agency for participatory knowing; ego strength sufficient to risk dissolution in order to expand perception.
trigger_scenario: A client in therapy says, "I want to see the parts of myself I keep hidden, but I’m scared I’ll be overwhelmed. If you think I can handle it, I’ll look."
anchor_text: Show me, Lord, what my eyes alone can never see.
